rulings.Case Title: MINAKO TOGASHI VS. CHRISTINE ELI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Cross-Deft'S Motion To Strike Slapp
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 16, 2007, LINE 3. CROSS-DEFENDANT MINAKO TOGASHI'S Motion To Strike SLAPP IS GRANTED. THE STATEMENTS BY MS. TOGASHI INVOLVED A MATTER OF PUBLIC CONCERN AND ARE PROTECTED ACTIVITY. WILBANKS V WOLK (2004) 121 CAL APP 4TH 883, 894; PARADISE HILLS ASSOC. V PROCEL (1991) 235 CAL APP 3RD 1528. THE STATEMENTS MADE WERE RELATED TO HER PENDING LAWSUIT AND ARE PRIVILEGED. C.C. SECTION 47(B). AS A CONSEQUENCE, ELI CANNOT PREVAIL ON HER CLAIMS. ROHDE V WOLF (AUGUST 15, 2007) 07 C.D.O.S. 9583. TOGASHI'S COUNSEL MUST SUBMIT A DETAILED DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: BRIDGETTE L JOHNSON VS. ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL, A SUTTER AFFILIATE, A et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 16, 2007, LINE 11. DEFENDANT ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED A PRIMA FACIE CASE. (PLAINTIFF'S PERFORMANCE PROBLEMS PRE-DATE AND POST-DATE HER PREGNANCY AND ARE REFLECTED IN SEVERAL E-MAILS) ASSUMING SHE HAS, DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED A LEGITIMATE REASON FOR PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION (FOLEY DECLARATION, PARAGRAPH 44). PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL AND RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE THAT THE REASON WAS PRETEXTUAL. TROP V SONY PICTURES ENTERTAINMENT INC. (2005) 129 CAL APP 4TH 1133 HOLDS THAT FOLEY'S COMMENTS ARE NOT DIRECT EVIDENCE BUT ARE, AT MOST, ISOLATED COMMENTS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DR. SUSEN'S TESTIMONY IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT TO IDENTIFY THE OBJECTIONS THAT REQUIRE A RULING AND THE COURT WILL CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION AT THE HEARING. =(302/PJM/PB)
